text:
Ga (ガ, Ga) is one of the Heavens Arena Floor Masters. He only appears in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission. Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission 

Appearance

Ga's is a tall, broad man. His true appearance is unknown, as a gray gas mask always conceals his face with red lenses intended to look like a moth. His gas mask has three tubes that come down over the neckline of his cloak and fold up underneath. 

He is clad in a large white cloak and a white turban, underneath which he wears a coat and pants of the same dark green color and gloves and boots of the same greenish grey. The lining of his cloak has a design reminiscent of moth wings. 

Plot

The Last Mission

Ga is first seen in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission entering the arena at Heavens Arena as Cocco is announcing the start of the Battle Olympia, walking in the middle of the group of Floor Masters. 

Later, Ga is seen lifting weights over his shoulders in the training room with the other Floor Masters as they await the start of the Battle Olympia matches. Jed enters the room and swiftly knocks out all of the Floor Masters and injects them with his blood. As Jed is communicating with his cohorts on his earpiece, Ga is seen in the background, slumped over the ropes of the boxing ring. 

When Gon and Killua later get trapped in a room with Ga and eight of the other Floor Masters who are under the influence of On from Jed's blood, Killua knocks Hae back who goes flying into Ga and the two fighters fall to the floor. Ga and the other Floor Masters shamble after Gon and Killua in a trance even after repeatedly being knocked down, but the two boys manage to escape the room and leave the Floor Masters behind. 

Abilities & Powers

Not much is known about Ga's abilities, but the fact that he was a Floor Master is proof enough of his power and proficiency in Nen. Still, he was defeated single-handedly by Jed. A magazine held by Killua shows him knocking down an opponent with a double kick to the face, so it can be assumed he is skilled at hand-to-hand combat. 

Magazine Finishers

Flight (Hikou): Ga can hover in the air. 
Drop Kick (Doroppu Kikku): Ga kicks the opponent in the face with both feet after jumping up. 

Trivia

Ga means "moth" in Japanese.
